title: FEMS acceptance replay eval (FEMS-EVAL-001.4) (folded WP-1-FEMS-Acceptance-Replay-Eval)
goal: Define and enforce deterministic acceptance/validation replay for FEMS so a past MemoryPack composition can be reconstructed exactly and re-evaluated. Folded from WP-1-FEMS-Acceptance-Replay-Eval. Critical for the self-improvement loop's reproducibility story and the integration validator's audit path.
expected_diff_shape: New module src/backend/handshake_core/src/memory/replay_eval.rs (ReplayEvaluator + ReplayRequest + ReplayResult + ReplayDifference types). Tests src/backend/handshake_core/tests/replay_eval_tests.rs.
unblock_conditions: MT-141 (MemoryCapsule), MT-145 (CapsuleRecord persistence), MT-157 (bitemporal indexing - replay needs both temporal axes).
implementation_notes: Per WP-1-FEMS-Acceptance-Replay-Eval preserved_intent: 'Define and enforce deterministic acceptance/validation for FEMS Phase 1 delivery'. Per refinement D.3 'acceptance replay eval'. ReplayRequest: `{ capsule_id: Uuid, replay_as_of: AsOfQuery (from MT-157), expected_source_hash: String }`. ReplayResult: `{ replayed_capsule: MemoryCapsule, replay_difference: ReplayDifference, replay_succeeded: bool }`. ReplayDifference: `{ items_present_now_absent_then: Vec<Uuid>, items_absent_now_present_then: Vec<Uuid>, items_with_different_scores: Vec<(Uuid, f64, f64)> }`. ReplayEvaluator: `pub fn replay(req: ReplayRequest, recorder: &dyn CapsuleRecordReader, bitemporal: &dyn BitemporalAccessor, builder: &CapsuleBuilder) -> Result<ReplayResult, ReplayError>`. Flow: (1) load original CapsuleRecord via recorder, (2) use bitemporal.items_visible_at(replay_as_of) to recover the world state at original build time, (3) re-run CapsuleBuilder against that frozen world state with the original RetrievalPolicy, (4) compare resulting capsule to original by source_hash (exact match) and diff item-level if mismatch. replay_succeeded = (source_hash matches). Use this to validate (a) bitemporal indexing is correct, (b) CapsuleBuilder is deterministic given fixed inputs, (c) self-improvement-loop iterations can be exactly reproduced from EventLedger. Tests: replay of just-built capsule succeeds with empty ReplayDifference; replay after a hygiene invalidation detects the diff (item absent now present then); replay with wrong expected_source_hash returns ReplayError::HashMismatch; replay is deterministic across multiple invocations.
proof_commands: $env:CARGO_TARGET_DIR='D:/Projects/LLM projects/Handshake/Handshake_Artifacts/handshake-cargo-target'; cargo test --manifest-path src/backend/handshake_core/Cargo.toml --features test-utils --test fems_v1_integration_tests replay_eval -- --nocapture
risk_if_missed: Without replay eval the self-improvement loop cannot prove reproducibility and the integration validator cannot audit past loop iterations.
validator_focus: Replay is deterministic; ReplayDifference surfaces every divergence; bitemporal accessor is the source of truth for as-of state.

builder_handoff_correction_20260525.current_interpretation: Kernel Builder implementation handoff readiness only; no WP/Integration Validator pass or validator-reviewed completion is claimed.

lifecycle.ready_for_validation_reason: Kernel Builder believes implementation/supporting evidence is ready for Integration Validator handoff; validator review remains pending.

minimum_controls: Replay is deterministic given fixed AsOfQuery; non-determinism is a test failure.
ReplayDifference enumerates every divergence so diffs can be inspected.
Bitemporal accessor is the sole source of as-of state; replay cannot accidentally consume current FEMS state.

implementation_evidence.summary: MT-163 implemented deterministic FEMS acceptance replay evaluation. Replay loads the original CapsuleRecord, verifies expected_source_hash, reconstructs replay inputs from bitemporal as-of items, rebuilds a MemoryCapsule through CapsuleBuilder over a frozen retriever, and returns item membership/score diffs plus typed errors.

sources_checked: Temporal Workflow Definition determinism and replay constraints: https://docs.temporal.io/workflow-definition#deterministic-constraints
LangSmith dataset versioning and as_of evaluation docs: https://docs.langchain.com/langsmith/manage-datasets
Kurrent/EventStoreDB system projections over event streams: https://docs.kurrent.io/server/v24.10/features/projections/system
DBOS durable execution / replay-oriented durable workflows docs: https://docs.dbos.dev/
relevant_patterns_found: Replayable systems isolate non-deterministic external access from the replay path and compare rebuilt commands/results against recorded history.
Evaluation systems version datasets and run evaluations against explicit as-of versions rather than mutable latest state.
Event-sourced projections rebuild state from durable event streams and make replay drift inspectable.
reuse_opportunities: Reused CapsuleRecord audit/source_hash from MT-145 as the original replay record.
Reused BitemporalIndex/AsOfQuery from MT-157 as the frozen world-state source.
Reused CapsuleBuilder/RetrievalPolicy from MT-141 so replay exercises the same pack composition logic.
rejected_options: Rejected snapshot-only replay because it bypasses CapsuleRecord, BitemporalItem payloads, and CapsuleBuilder determinism.
Rejected using a caller-provided live CapsuleBuilder retriever because it can leak current FEMS state into replay.
Rejected adding a new persistence shape for original MemoryPack contents because MT-145 already defines CapsuleRecord as the durable record.
research_basis.selected_approach: Accept the contract builder handle but rebuild through a scoped CapsuleBuilder bound to ReplayFrozenFemsRetriever populated only from bitemporal.items_visible_at(replay_as_of). Compare original audit snapshot against replayed capsule audit and source_hash.
risks: ReplayDifference uses Vec<Uuid> per MT-163 contract; non-UUID audit item IDs fail closed.

PostgresBitemporalMemoryIndex is async while the MT-163 BitemporalAccessor trait is sync; this pass covers the in-memory production BitemporalIndex adapter and leaves async adapter wiring to a later integration layer if needed.
Score-only drift may not change MemoryCapsule.source_hash because source_hash is based on pack items, so replay_succeeded must check both source_hash and ReplayDifference.
mitigations: InvalidRecordItemId is a typed fail-closed ReplayError for non-UUID audit IDs.
BitemporalAccessor is a narrow trait so a blocking/async bridge can be added without changing ReplayEvaluator semantics.
Tests assert score drift returns replay_succeeded=false even if the pack hash can remain unchanged.
validation_plan: Run the dedicated replay_eval_tests integration binary.
Run the FEMS V1 integration replay smoke against the new contract.
Run rustfmt --check, git diff --check, and forbidden SQLite/model-runtime scans on touched files.

red.note: Initial red run failed against the snapshot-only replay_eval stub: CapsuleRecordReader lacked read_record, BitemporalAccessor lacked items_visible_at, ReplayResult.replayed_capsule was ReplaySnapshot instead of MemoryCapsule, and ReplayEvaluator::replay lacked the contract builder parameter.

green.note: 5 passed: just-built capsule replay, invalidation diff, wrong expected hash typed error, deterministic repeated replay, score drift detection.

out_of_owned_files_pending_operator_revi: src/backend/handshake_core/tests/fems_v1_integration_tests.rs (updated legacy replay smoke from snapshot stub API to CapsuleRecord + bitemporal replay API)
high_roi_additions: Blanket CapsuleRecordReader impl for MemoryCapsuleIpcStore so existing capsule stores can serve replay records.
BitemporalAccessor impl for in-memory BitemporalIndex so the production as-of filter is tested directly.
Typed InvalidBitemporalPayload and InvalidRecordItemId errors to fail closed on malformed replay inputs.
Score-drift test and success hardening so non-empty ReplayDifference cannot be reported as successful.

proof.note: 5 passed; includes real in-memory BitemporalIndex adapter coverage.

residual_risks: No Integration Validator has reviewed MT-163; READY_FOR_VALIDATION is implementer handoff only.
The async PostgresBitemporalMemoryIndex path remains outside this microtask proof because the contract trait is sync; validation may request an adapter or ignored Postgres integration in a follow-up.
UUID-only diff vectors are contract-compliant but require replayable capsule audit IDs to be UUID strings.

integration_validator_review.verdict_reason: ReplayEvaluator implements the contracted shape: loads CapsuleRecord via CapsuleRecordReader, validates expected_source_hash with typed ReplayError::HashMismatch, recovers as-of state via BitemporalAccessor.items_visible_at, rebuilds capsule via CapsuleBuilder over a frozen retriever, and returns ReplayResult with item-level diff plus replay_succeeded boolean (true iff source_hash matches AND ReplayDifference empty). 5 + 1 integration tests pass per builder. Two notes: BitemporalAccessor trait is sync while production PostgresBitemporalMemoryIndex is async (builder flagged this as residual risk — adapter wiring deferred); ReplayDifference uses Vec<Uuid> per contract, requiring UUID audit-ids — non-UUID ids hard-fail-closed via InvalidRecordItemId.

Two notes: BitemporalAccessor trait is sync while production PostgresBitemporalMemoryIndex is async (builder flagged this as residual risk — adapter wiring deferred); ReplayDifference uses Vec<Uuid> per contract, requiring UUID audit-ids — non-UUID ids hard-fail-closed via InvalidRecordItemId.
